inline_comments.message: Does istio insist on a label called "app"?  Couldn't it use app.kubernetes.io/name?

in my patch (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/128371)
I tried it in the "matchLabels" to use something different than "app" without success:

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: {{ include "common.servicename" . }}
  namespace: {{ include "common.namespace" . }}
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: {{ include "common.servicename" . }}
  portLevelMtls:
    {{ .Values.service.internalPort }}:
      mode: DISABLE

But I will have to check, if the label "app" could only be used for "service" and not generally, as currently for some reason CPS has a problem.
It seems "deployment" definition has a problem with the label "app":
root@control01-istio:/opt/oom/kubernetes# helm upgrade -i onap-cps local/cps --namespace onap --version 10.0.0 --values /opt/oom/kubernetes/onap/resources/overrides/onap-all.yaml --values /opt/oom/kubernetes/onap/resources/overrides/environment.yaml --values /home/ubuntu/oom/master/onap-overrides.yaml --values /home/ubuntu/oom/master/onap-components.yaml --set config.useStrimziKafka="true" --timeout '900s'
Release "onap-cps" does not exist. Installing it now.
Error: Deployment.apps "onap-ncmp-dmi-plugin" is invalid: spec.template.metadata.labels: Invalid value: map[string]string{"app.kubernetes.io/instance":"onap", "app.kubernetes.io/name":"ncmp-dmi-plugin"}: `selector` does not match template `labels`
